author: Labhdi Bijayaji
title: Dayanand Kutark Nirnai
gloss: This pamphlet tries to refute the objections against Jainism contained in the Sattyarth Prakash of Swami Dayanand, the founder of the Arya Samaj.
notes: Jain rebuttal of Dayanand's Satyarth Prakash — Jain voice in the Punjab polemical arena.

title: Maut ke bad kya hota hai
gloss: A lecture on what takes place after death, adapted from the theosophical works of Leadbeater, by Rai Bishambar Nath of Delhi.
copyright: Rai Bishambhar Nath, Delhi; No. 153, dated the 22nd September, 1910.
notes: Leadbeater's Theosophy in Urdu — same Theosophist compiler as the Blavatsky biography (p26 s32).

title: Miftah-ul-A'mal
gloss: An adapted translation by the Revd. Dr. H. U. Wightbrecht, Ph. D., D. D. of Canon Morris' Key to the Acts, with Appendicies.

author: Muhammad Abdul Aziz, Maulvi
title: Aziz-ul-Akhirat
gloss: A last will which the author on his death bed addresses to his two sons, enjoining upon them strict observance of the Muhammadan religious precepts, with other admonitions for their guidance.
copyright: M. Abdur Rahman and M. Ahmad Abdullah, Deccan, No. 97, dated the 7th July, 1910.
notes: Dual date: 1326 Hijri. Hyderabad-printed book registered in Punjab; copyright held by the sons addressed in the will.

author: Muhammad Haidarullah Khan, Maulvi
title: Al-Atban ala Imámat-il-Umyan
gloss: A pamphlet in which the writer tries to prove that to perform prayers behind a blind man is forbidden by the Muhammadan law.

author: Muhammad Salih, Maulvi
title: Silsila-i-Islam, Hissa-i-Duwum. Najaston ka bayan
gloss: Book No. 2 of a series entitled the Silsila-i-Islam, on impurities, their effect and how to remove them.

author: Muhammad Salih, Maulvi
title: Hissa-i-Siwum. Ghusl ka bayan
notes: Author dash-continuation of serial 69; part 3 of the Silsila-i-Islam series. Dual date: 1328 Hijri.

title: Nai Ginti
gloss: Tract No. 33-3 in which the numerals from 1 to 20 are taught and along with them Muhammadan precepts, such as God is one, the main principles of Islam are two, viz., belief in God and the profession of that belief, etc.
notes: Numeracy primer doubling as catechism — religious pedagogy through arithmetic.
